data.title: Will Shrinking Volumes and Margin Pressure at CAGNY Change Molson Coors Beverage's (TAP) Narrative
data.description:
• In February 2026, Molson Coors Beverage released its 2025 fourth-quarter and full-year results and outlined its 2026 medium-term outlook at the CAGNY Conference, opting not to hold a separate quarterly earnings call.
  • At the same time, analysts highlighted shrinking unit sales, margin pressure from higher expenses, and a reliance on pricing and brand extensions to sustain Molson Coors’ position as a slower-growth but income-focused consumer staples name.
  • We’ll now examine how concerns over shrinking unit sales and margin pressure could shape Molson Coors’ existing investment narrative and outlook.

Molson Coors Beverage Investment Narrative Recap

To own Molson Coors today, you have to be comfortable with a slower-moving, income-oriented beverage company that is working through weak volumes and margin pressure. The February 2026 results and CAGNY outlook are unlikely to change the near term focus on stabilizing unit sales and protecting profitability, while the biggest current risk remains further erosion in beer demand and returns if volume declines persist.

The upcoming 2025 full year release and 2026 medium term outlook at CAGNY matter most because they directly address the recent pattern of shrinking volumes and higher expenses. With analysts already pointing to weaker operating margins and negative returns on capital, what management says about cost discipline, brand extensions and pricing power will be central to how investors reassess both the income story and the risk that margins stay under pressure.

Yet beneath Molson Coors’ “boring but safe” reputation, investors should be aware of the growing risk that sustained volume declines and rising costs could...

Howell.

That background ties closely to NRG’s capital intensive push into additional gas capacity and data center related load, where disciplined project selection, balance sheet management and alignment with evolving ESG expectations could be critical for how the existing investment story plays out.

data.title: Suncorp Group Limited (ASX:SUN) Stock's Been Sliding But Fundamentals Look Decent: Will The Market Correct The Share Price In The Future?
data.description:
Suncorp Group (ASX:SUN) has had a rough three months with its share price down 16%. However, stock prices are usually driven by a company’s financials over the long term, which in this case look pretty respectable. Specifically, we decided to study Suncorp Group's ROE in this article.

Return on equity or ROE is an important factor to be considered by a shareholder because it tells them how effectively their capital is being reinvested. In other words, it is a profitability ratio which measures the rate of return on the capital provided by the company's shareholders.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

How To Calculate Return On Equity?

ROE can be calculated by using the formula:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for Suncorp Group is:

15% = AU$1.6b ÷ AU$11b (Based on the trailing twelve months to June 2025).

The 'return' is the amount earned after tax over the last twelve months. So, this means that for every A$1 of its shareholder's investments, the company generates a profit of A$0.15.

View our latest analysis for Suncorp Group

What Is The Relationship Between ROE And Earnings Growth?

So far, we've learned that ROE is a measure of a company's profitability. We now need to evaluate how much profit the company reinvests or "retains" for future growth which then gives us an idea about the growth potential of the company. Generally speaking, other things being equal, firms with a high return on equity and profit retention, have a higher growth rate than firms that don’t share these attributes.

Suncorp Group's Earnings Growth And 15% ROE

To begin with, Suncorp Group seems to have a respectable ROE. Even when compared to the industry average of 15% the company's ROE looks quite decent. This certainly adds some context to Suncorp Group's moderate 12% net income growth seen over the past five years.

We then compared Suncorp Group's net income growth with the industry and found that the company's growth figure is lower than the average industry growth rate of 19% in the same 5-year period, which is a bit concerning.

The basis for attaching value to a company is, to a great extent, tied to its earnings growth. It’s important for an investor to know whether the market has priced in the company's expected earnings growth (or decline). This then helps them determine if the stock is placed for a bright or bleak future. Has the market priced in the future outlook for SUN? You can find out in our latest intrinsic value infographic research report.

Is Suncorp Group Using Its Retained Earnings Effectively?

While Suncorp Group has a three-year median payout ratio of 78% (which means it retains 22% of profits), the company has still seen a fair bit of earnings growth in the past, meaning that its high payout ratio hasn't hampered its ability to grow.

Moreover, Suncorp Group is determined to keep sharing its profits with shareholders which we infer from its long history of paying a dividend for at least ten years. Our latest analyst data shows that the future payout ratio of the company over the next three years is expected to be approximately 73%. As a result, Suncorp Group's ROE is not expected to change by much either, which we inferred from the analyst estimate of 13% for future ROE.

Summary

On the whole, we do feel that Suncorp Group has some positive attributes. Its earnings growth is decent, and the high ROE does contribute to that growth. However, investors could have benefitted even more from the high ROE, had the company been reinvesting more of its earnings. Having said that, on studying current analyst estimates, we were concerned to see that while the company has grown its earnings in the past, analysts expect its earnings to shrink in the future. To know more about the latest analysts predictions for the company, check out this visualization of analyst forecasts for the company.

data.title: Subdued Growth No Barrier To Michael Hill International Limited (ASX:MHJ) With Shares Advancing 25%
data.description:
Michael Hill International Limited (ASX:MHJ) shares have had a really impressive month, gaining 25% after a shaky period beforehand. Unfortunately, the gains of the last month did little to right the losses of the last year with the stock still down 21% over that time.

Even after such a large jump in price, it's still not a stretch to say that Michael Hill International's price-to-sales (or "P/S") ratio of 0.3x right now seems quite "middle-of-the-road" compared to the Specialty Retail industry in Australia, where the median P/S ratio is around 0.6x. While this might not raise any eyebrows, if the P/S ratio is not justified investors could be missing out on a potential opportunity or ignoring looming disappointment.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

View our latest analysis for Michael Hill International

Advertisement

How Michael Hill International Has Been Performing

While the industry has experienced revenue growth lately, Michael Hill International's revenue has gone into reverse gear, which is not great. One possibility is that the P/S ratio is moderate because investors think this poor revenue performance will turn around. You'd really hope so, otherwise you're paying a relatively elevated price for a company with this sort of growth profile.

If you'd like to see what analysts are forecasting going forward, you should check out our free report on Michael Hill International.

How Is Michael Hill International's Revenue Growth Trending?

Michael Hill International's P/S ratio would be typical for a company that's only expected to deliver moderate growth, and importantly, perform in line with the industry.

Retrospectively, the last year delivered virtually the same number to the company's top line as the year before. Regardless, revenue has managed to lift by a handy 8.4% in aggregate from three years ago, thanks to the earlier period of growth. Therefore, it's fair to say that revenue growth has been inconsistent recently for the company.

Shifting to the future, estimates from the three analysts covering the company suggest revenue should grow by 2.6% per annum over the next three years. Meanwhile, the rest of the industry is forecast to expand by 11% each year, which is noticeably more attractive.

In light of this, it's curious that Michael Hill International's P/S sits in line with the majority of other companies. Apparently many investors in the company are less bearish than analysts indicate and aren't willing to let go of their stock right now. These shareholders may be setting themselves up for future disappointment if the P/S falls to levels more in line with the growth outlook.

The Bottom Line On Michael Hill International's P/S

Michael Hill International's stock has a lot of momentum behind it lately, which has brought its P/S level with the rest of the industry. Typically, we'd caution against reading too much into price-to-sales ratios when settling on investment decisions, though it can reveal plenty about what other market participants think about the company.

Given that Michael Hill International's revenue growth projections are relatively subdued in comparison to the wider industry, it comes as a surprise to see it trading at its current P/S ratio. At present, we aren't confident in the P/S as the predicted future revenues aren't likely to support a more positive sentiment for long. Circumstances like this present a risk to current and prospective investors who may see share prices fall if the low revenue growth impacts the sentiment.

Before you settle on your opinion, we've discovered 1 warning sign for Michael Hill International that you should be aware of.

If you're unsure about the strength of Michael Hill International's business, why not explore our interactive list of stocks with solid business fundamentals for some other companies you may have missed.

data.title: Healius Limited (ASX:HLS) Looks Inexpensive But Perhaps Not Attractive Enough
data.description:
When you see that almost half of the companies in the Healthcare industry in Australia have price-to-sales ratios (or "P/S") above 1.1x, Healius Limited (ASX:HLS) looks to be giving off some buy signals with its 0.5x P/S ratio. However, the P/S might be low for a reason and it requires further investigation to determine if it's justified.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Check out our latest analysis for Healius

Advertisement

What Does Healius' Recent Performance Look Like?

With revenue growth that's inferior to most other companies of late, Healius has been relatively sluggish. It seems that many are expecting the uninspiring revenue performance to persist, which has repressed the growth of the P/S ratio. If this is the case, then existing shareholders will probably struggle to get excited about the future direction of the share price.

If you'd like to see what analysts are forecasting going forward, you should check out our free report on Healius.

What Are Revenue Growth Metrics Telling Us About The Low P/S?

In order to justify its P/S ratio, Healius would need to produce sluggish growth that's trailing the industry.

Retrospectively, the last year delivered a decent 5.7% gain to the company's revenues. However, this wasn't enough as the latest three year period has seen an unpleasant 41% overall drop in revenue. Therefore, it's fair to say the revenue growth recently has been undesirable for the company.

Shifting to the future, estimates from the ten analysts covering the company suggest revenue should grow by 4.4% per annum over the next three years. Meanwhile, the rest of the industry is forecast to expand by 20% per year, which is noticeably more attractive.

In light of this, it's understandable that Healius' P/S sits below the majority of other companies. Apparently many shareholders weren't comfortable holding on while the company is potentially eyeing a less prosperous future.

The Final Word

We'd say the price-to-sales ratio's power isn't primarily as a valuation instrument but rather to gauge current investor sentiment and future expectations.

As we suspected, our examination of Healius' analyst forecasts revealed that its inferior revenue outlook is contributing to its low P/S. Shareholders' pessimism on the revenue prospects for the company seems to be the main contributor to the depressed P/S. It's hard to see the share price rising strongly in the near future under these circumstances.

The company's balance sheet is another key area for risk analysis. You can assess many of the main risks through our free balance sheet analysis for Healius with six simple checks.

Of course, profitable companies with a history of great earnings growth are generally safer bets. So you may wish to see this free collection of other companies that have reasonable P/E ratios and have grown earnings strongly.

data.title: Assessing Huabao International Holdings (SEHK:336) Valuation After The Recent CEO Transition
data.description:
Huabao International Holdings (SEHK:336) is back in focus after the board announced a CEO transition, with long serving leader Ms. Chu Lam Yiu stepping down from the role and Mr. Xia Liqun taking over.

See our latest analysis for Huabao International Holdings.

The CEO handover comes after a period of strong short term momentum, with the 7 day share price return of 11.53% and 90 day share price return of 18.67%. However, the 1 year total shareholder return of 122.44% contrasts with a far weaker 5 year total shareholder return of a 50.89% decline, which hints that sentiment has improved recently but longer term holders have still faced a tougher journey.

If this kind of leadership change has you rethinking your watchlist, it could be a good moment to broaden your search with fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With the share price already up sharply over the past year, but the company posting a HK$297.09 million loss and screening with a low value score, you have to ask: is Huabao undervalued today, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Advertisement

Price-to-Sales of 3.8x: Is it justified?

Huabao International Holdings trades on a P/S of 3.8x, while the last close was HK$4.45, and that compares with far lower peer and industry levels.

The P/S ratio compares the company’s market value to its annual revenue, so for Huabao you are paying 3.8 times its reported sales. For a business that is currently loss making, investors often look at P/S because earnings based measures like P/E are not meaningful.

Here, the market is assigning Huabao a much richer revenue multiple than both its Hong Kong Chemicals industry average of 0.5x and its peer group at 2.1x. That suggests buyers today are paying a premium price for each dollar of sales relative to what is typical across the sector and among closer peers.

Put simply, Huabao’s shares are priced at more than 7 times the industry’s P/S and well above the peer average, which indicates a premium stance rather than a discount.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-sales of 3.8x (OVERVALUED)

However, you also have a recent HK$297.09 million loss and a value score of 0, which could pressure sentiment if the transition to the new CEO disappoints investors.

Find out about the key risks to this Huabao International Holdings narrative.

Another View: SWS DCF model also flags rich pricing

Our DCF model points in the same direction as the P/S check, with Huabao International Holdings at HK$4.45 compared with an estimated fair value of HK$2.67. That gap suggests limited room for error if the business does not turn its loss into sustainable profits. Where do you think the pressure point sits: the share price or the fundamentals?

With JTEKT trading at ¥1,803, sitting close to an analyst price target of ¥1,837.50 but at a reported intrinsic discount of about 60%, you have to ask whether there is still a buying opportunity here or if the market is already pricing in future growth.

Price-to-Earnings of 29.2x: Is it justified?

On earnings multiples, JTEKT looks expensive at a P/E of 29.2x compared with both its peer group and the wider JP auto components industry, despite the last close of ¥1,803.

The P/E ratio compares the current share price to earnings per share, so a higher figure generally reflects the market paying more for each unit of current earnings.

For JTEKT, that 29.2x P/E sits well above the peer average of 11.8x and the JP Auto Components industry average of 10.7x. This gap suggests the market is pricing in much stronger earnings than those implied for its peers. Relative to an estimated fair P/E of 22.2x, the current multiple is also higher. This points to a valuation level that could move closer to that fair ratio if sentiment or expectations change.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for JTEKT

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 29.2x (OVERVALUED)

Separately, our DCF model points to a very different picture for JTEKT, with a fair value estimate of ¥4,543.76 per share versus the current ¥1,803, implying the shares are trading well below that modelled value.

The SWS DCF model estimates future cash flows over time and discounts them back to today using a required rate of return. This produces a single fair value number from a series of projected cash flows.

For a diversified auto components and machinery group like JTEKT, where earnings and free cash flow can be influenced by capital intensity and one off items, a DCF framework can sometimes capture long term cash generation in a different way to simple earnings multiples.

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

Result: DCF Fair value of ¥4,543.76 (UNDERVALUED)

However, you still need to watch for any shift in analyst assumptions behind that DCF and for sector wide pressures that could challenge current earnings expectations.

Find out about the key risks to this JTEKT narrative.

Another View: DCF Paints a Very Different Picture

While the 29.2x P/E suggests JTEKT is expensive compared to peers and the fair ratio, our DCF model points the other way. With a fair value estimate of ¥4,543.76 per share versus the current ¥1,803, it presents the shares as materially undervalued. Which lens do you trust more?

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

You can track the result in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted when this changes, or use our stock screener to discover 885 undervalued stocks based on their cash flows. If you save a screener we even alert you when new companies match - so you never miss a potential opportunity.

If you see the numbers differently, or simply prefer to test your own assumptions against the data, you can build a full view in just a few minutes, starting with Do it your way.

data.title: Did Surging CAR Options Volatility and Bearish Analyst Signals Just Shift Avis Budget Group's Investment Narrative?
data.description:
• In recent days, options traders have driven up implied volatility on January US$80 call options for Avis Budget Group, while analysts have maintained a generally negative earnings outlook, including a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell) rating.
  • This combination of heightened options activity and cautious analyst sentiment highlights rising uncertainty around potential near-term developments for Avis’s business.
  • We’ll now examine how this elevated options-implied volatility may influence Avis Budget Group’s existing investment narrative and future expectations.

Advertisement

Avis Budget Group Investment Narrative Recap

To own Avis Budget Group, you need to believe in its ability to turn an established rental brand and fleet platform into a more profitable, tech-enabled mobility business. The spike in implied volatility around the January US$80 calls, alongside cautious earnings views, points to uncertainty around near term newsflow, but does not fundamentally alter the central catalyst of improving profitability or the key risk of earnings pressure and balance sheet strain.

Against this backdrop, the long running share buyback program, which has retired over 83,626,152 shares for about US$7,356.97 million since 2013, looks particularly relevant. With options pricing hinting at bigger short term swings, that history of capital returns sits alongside the more immediate concern that recent losses and weaker interest coverage could limit financial flexibility just when the market is focused on near term shocks.

Yet behind the options volatility, investors should be aware of how rising losses could constrain...

data.title: Citigroup’s Backing of Tower-Focused Strategy Might Change The Case For Investing In Array Digital (AD)
data.description:
• Earlier this month, Citigroup initiated and reinforced positive research coverage on Array Digital Infrastructure, highlighting the company’s repositioning around its tower assets and retained C-band spectrum following recent asset sales to major U.S. carriers.
  • This research attention underscores how Array Digital’s shift from regional wireless operations toward infrastructure and spectrum holdings is reshaping how investors assess its business profile and risks.
  • We’ll now examine how Citi’s supportive view of Array Digital’s tower-focused model influences the company’s existing investment narrative and risk‑reward balance.

We've found 12 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

Array Digital Infrastructure Investment Narrative Recap

To own Array Digital Infrastructure, you need to be comfortable with a business that is pivoting away from regional wireless operations toward towers and retained spectrum, with Citi’s new coverage reinforcing that infrastructure‑centric thesis. In the near term, the most important catalyst remains execution on the tower revenue opportunity, while the biggest risk is still the timing and certainty of proceeds tied to the remaining carrier transactions; Citi’s positive stance does not materially change that risk profile.

Among recent announcements, the US$23.00 per share special dividend in August 2025 stands out in this context, as it highlighted how asset sale proceeds could be returned to shareholders while the company refocused on its tower and spectrum platform. For investors tracking catalysts, that payout framed Array Digital’s capital allocation approach alongside Citi’s focus on the evolving, tower‑led model and the balance between reinvestment, debt reduction, and distributions.

Yet against this constructive setup, the reliance on a single large carrier transaction still creates a concentration risk that investors should be aware of...

Read the full narrative on Array Digital Infrastructure (it's free!)

Array Digital Infrastructure's narrative projects $3.6 billion revenue and $173.7 million earnings by 2028. This implies a 0.8% yearly revenue decline but an earnings increase of about $212.7 million from -$39.0 million today.

Uncover how Array Digital Infrastructure's forecasts yield a $54.50 fair value, a 3% upside to its current price.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Two fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community cluster tightly between US$54.50 and US$59.32, showing how closely some private investors are sizing up Array Digital today. Set against Citi’s attention on the tower business and the execution risk around pending carrier transactions, these differing viewpoints remind you to weigh several perspectives on what might drive the company’s future performance.
